feed.title: Accessibility Wars - sword
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: The Blast and the Quickness [pDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build has no kits and provides reliable DPS and an excessive amount of quickness with large amounts of superspeed.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Dragon's
Ring Dragon's
Relic Claw
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Dragon's
Ring Dragon's
Relic Claw

Consumables:

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMmHwY3Ky0qD4QAJgEmAa4SAAAnEwAAiQGJAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgA2AFUAAA==]

GW2Skills Link

Notes

Granting Quickness and Superspeed

You give quickness through the trait Kinetic Accelerators by performing blast, leap or whirl combos when a field is active. Your field skills are

  1. Blast Gyro
  2. Function Gyro
  3. Spare Capacitor (no superspeed)
  • Pop your kit fields when lacking before using combos.
  • Your field gyros provide superspeed but Spare Capacitor does not. If you use Bypass Coating with Spare Capacitor, it will increase your superspeed uptime and hence increase your DPS.
  • Essentially if you don't want to memorise rotations, if you sweep from right to left (98754321)
  • If you mashed all your field skills and run out of them, you can create one using Elite Mortar Kit, although this is almost never necessary.
  • Other elite skills can provide additional utility/defiance break if you do not require the Elite Mortar Kit.
  • You can also use Function Gyro if you have allies that have been downed, and this will summon up to 3 seperate Gyros, which will automatically go revive allies nearby.

DPS Rotation

  1. Start this after providing quickness
  2. Blowtorch
  3. Refraction Cutter
  4. Auto-attack

Crowd Control

CC skills

  1. Throw Mine (used in DPS rotation)
  2. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt - backup)
  3. Spare Capacitor (CC + Lightning Field)
  4. Radiant Arc (Lightning Field Leap Combo)
  5. Supply Crate(Optional Elite)

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: 0-KIT ALAC-T1002-P [aDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build has no kits and provides reliable DPS and Alacrity. The kits can be added back for additional DPS when one is comfortable with the build.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Diviner's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Diviner's
Ring Diviner's
Relic Claw
  • Start by adding Diviner's gear until your boon duration is about 20-25%. Fill the rest with Dragon's and Berserker's gear.
  • Be careful not to overcap crit due to High Caliber.

Other weapons

Shield Berserker's Air

This can be used if more CC is desired for a small DPS loss.

Food and Utility and Infusions

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMmHwY7SyoqD4QAjgEmASYBAABaHQAAex2JAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgA2AFUAAA==]

GW2Skills Link

Rotation

Granting Alacrity

You give Alacrity with Morph Skills:

  1. Offensive Protocol: Obliterate
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce
  3. Defensive Protocol: Thorns
  • You should use these skills spread out but frequently enough to ensure Alacrity doesn't drop.
  • Offensive Protocol: Pierce is important to upkeep  Relic of the Claw

DPS Rotation

  1. Open with Rocket Turret and DO NOT Detonate Rocket Turret
  2. Use all your skills off-cooldown
  3. Spread out morph skills to maintain Willing Host
  4. Spread out CC skills to maintain  Relic of the Claw

CC skills

  1. Explosive Rockets (used in opener)
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce (used in DPS rotation)
  3. Throw Mine (used in DPS rotation)
  4. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt - backup)

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: Venom [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to use a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. Condition damage and eight-legged pets are empowered through the trait Arachnophobia. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Ritualist's
Amulet Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Ritualist's
Ring Ritualist's
Relic Fractal
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Ritualist's Malice
Torch Ritualist's Doom
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Axe Ritualist's Malice
Dagger Ritualist's Geomancy

Consumables

  •  Salsa-Topped Veggie Flatbread
  •  Magnanimous Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQQeHSE3SB95AHkAvwClAb0AAAC6ABsADxvtAAYkKwEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADBQAvAGYAAA==]
Juvenile Carrion Devourer
Juvenile Black Widow Spider

Rotation

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation
  4. Repeat

Precast

  1. Sharpening Stone

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. All your Utility Skills (7/8/9)
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst (Essential Unleashed Pet F1)
    • Rending Vines (Optional F2)
    • Enveloping Haze (Optional F3)
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you just have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleashed Ambush to proceed with the quickness rotation.
  • The 3 pet skills have no cast time but venomous outburst is your biggest contributor. The other 2 may be skipped to lower your APM if they slow your rotation. The damage difference is negligible.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, can just use F2.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Neurotoxin Burst OR
    • Sundering Volley
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation - L to R (A/D) R to L (D/T)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> use the skills from left to right for Axe/Dagger and from right to left for Dagger/Torch. The first skill you use will be recharged faster. This skill is bolded in the rotation and should be cast twice before Unleash Pet and reentering the opener. This will maximise the effect of Quick Draw. This is bolded in the rotation below.

  • Dagger/Torch 5432 then 5
    1. Skill 5
    2. Skill 4
    3. Skill 3
    4. Skill 2
    5. Skill 5
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Axe/Dagger 2345 then 2
    1. Skill 2
    2. Skill 3
    3. Skill 4
    4. Skill 5
    5. Skill 2
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired.
  • Any condition based spider or devourer pet is preferred due to Arachnophobia
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Using Unleash Ranger too early will lead to a large DPS loss. Using it late will lead to a small DPS loss and maintain quickness.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Tail Lash (F1)
  2. Exploding Spores (Utility Alternative)
  3. Spike Trap (Utility Alternative)
  4. Bring other pets if you need more

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Whirling Bunny Thumper [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to activate the  Relic of the Claw to reduce relic downtime on burst windows on a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. This provides steady quickness for the group and decent sustained CC. There is a relatively a large amount of button downtime filled with auto attacks. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

There are two channeled skills that will destroy your build when cancelled. These skills are positioned in the rotation where they are less likely to be cancelled (because the other skills are on cooldown).

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Claw
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Berserker's Air
Hydromancy
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Air
Axe Berserker's Hydromancy

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQQgHx45SBd5AHkAARsAAKUBAACsAQAADxsAAEAQARMAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADMwBaAAUAAA==]
Juvenile Black Moa
Juvenile Wallow

Rotation

Start in Unleash Pet using Sword/Axe

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. "Sic 'Em!"
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst
    • Rending Vines
    • Enveloping Haze
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleash Ambush to give quickness.
  • Starting the opener with sword will trigger  Relic of the Claw right at the beginning which is a significant initial DPS burst.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, you press the three pet skills one by one as fast as you can.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Relentless Whirl OR
    • Deft Strike
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation (2/3/4/5/7/8)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> Autos about to start/started

  1. Weapon 2
    • Unleashed Wild Swing OR Pounce
  2. Weapon 3
    • Unleashed Overbearing Smash OR Serpent's Strike
  3. Weapon 4
    • Unleashed Savage Shock Wave OR Path of Scars
  4. Weapon 5
    • Unleashed Thump OR Whirling Defense
  5. Utilities 7/8 if available +/- Pet skill (F3)
    • Exploding Spores OR "Sic 'Em!"
    • Undead Plague
  6. Optional Weapon 2 on refresh
  7. Autos (till 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes)
  8. Unleash Pet -> Unleash Ranger
  9. Continue with Quickness Rotation
  • Use "Sic 'Em!" on cooldown or during a comfortable window when you are not pressing any skills.
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired. Any power based pet is viable.
  • Try not to cancel Relentless Whirl or Whirling Defense.
  • You may wish to introduce a slight delay after casting Relentless Whirl
  • The sequence of button presses are similar on both weapons.
  • If you are slow on your rotation, you can skip Weapon 3.
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Dragging your feet on using ambush will lead to a small 1-2k DPS loss. Swapping Unleash Mode too early will lead to a higher DPS loss.

Relic of the Claw Uptime

< Relic of the Claw is triggered by your CC skills. These are littered between your important skill casts except during the end of the hammer phase. If you have followed the DPS rotation above Exploding Spores should be available towards the later part of your hammer DPS rotation.

If you started off with the wrong weapon, then remember to reserve Exploding Spores for hammer.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Deft Strike before Relentless Whirl
  2. Path of Scars before Whirling Defense
  3. Exploding Spores during hammer rotation
  4. Your pet F3 skills deal CC through the trait Beastly Warden

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Boon Power Chronomancer [Boon DPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Power Boon Chronomancer uses the exact same rotation concept as Power Chronomancer. By simply swapping 1 trait, we are able to provide either full Quickness OR Alacrity uptime to our subgroup, while also contributing decent damage.

It provides quickness/alacrity through the use of Phantasms or Shatter skills, both of which are already incorporated into the base Power Chronomancer build.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQcBHQoaKAYjDwAAjwEAAM8SAACCAQAAsxIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAvAAA=]

Select Stretched Time if you want to give Alacrity, or Seize the Moment if you want to give Quickness.

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  4. Signet of the Ether
  5. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  6. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  7. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Signet of the Ether instantly recharges the cooldown of any Phantasmal type skill, and is used to deal more damage.

Use Well of Action off cooldown, as it activates Danger Time, giving you 10% more critical danage. Never delay Well of Action.

Your cooldowns will line up in such a way that you will always be able to pair Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) with either Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) or Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) off cooldown, pairing it with the accompanying skill that is off cooldown.

Every few rotations, your cooldowns will line up such that you will be able to cast the following combo:

Burst Combo:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Signet of the Ether
  4. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  5. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  6. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Which is basically your opener minus the first Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use your burst combo as soon as it is available.

On top of this, you should also use Split Second off cooldown, after the first cast. You should wait until you have 3 clones before casting Split Second for the first time.

Split Second has no cast point, can be casted at the same time as other skills, and will not interrupt any other skill you are currently casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Gravity Well (BIG CC)
  2. Illusionary Leap > Swap (Sword 3)
  3. Time Sink (more clones more CC)

In most situations, just using Gravity Well would be enough contribution to break the CC bar.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can also block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where its cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Video

entry.title: Power Troubadour [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Melee Power build with high burst damage, ability to skip mechanics, and option to swap out utility for much more CC.

 

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
Berserker's Infiltration
Assassin's Infiltration
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Assassin's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone
 

Build

[&DQcBHQoaSScjDwAAaQEAAIIBAABmHQAAbR0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAyAAA=]
 

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  2. Lively Lute
  3. Flustering Flute
  4. Deafening Drum
  5. Crescendo

Your opener is also your burst rotation, and should be used whenever Crescendo is coming off cooldown.

Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) and Lively Lute have the same cooldown, and should always be used together. This is your highest priority and should not be delayed for anything else, and it should be extended into the burst rotation when Crescendo is available.

Take note that you do want to use Lively Lute with 3 Notes active for maximum damage, but by following this rotation you will always have 3 Notes when casting Lively Lute, removing one source of resource management.

On top of this, you should:

Use off cooldown:

  1. Power Spike (Only use 1 charge)
  2. Blurred Frenzy
  3. Tale of the Tortured Mastermind

In that order of priority. Power Spike has no cast time and can be used during the cast of another skill without interrupting it.

 

Tale of the August Queen is a 2 second long immune skill, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where it's cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Illusionary Leap --> Swap
  2. Deafening Drum

and you can swap out Tale of the August Queen for Signet of Humility if a lot of CC is required for the encounter.

 

Video

entry.title: Dual Blademaster [DPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This is a build which uses mostly Vipers and Grieving gear to maximise the dual swords' physical prowress and bleeding capabilities.

This build has a lot of auto attacks.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Grieving Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
EquipmentStatRune/Sigil
Sword (MH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Torment
Sword (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
All TrinketsViper'sN/A
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Fractal
Relic (DPS)N/ARelic of the Thorn
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Biomancer
Relic (Sust)N/ARelic of the Mistfire

Optional weapon sets

EquipmentStatSigil
Mace (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth
Dagger (OH)Viper's Superior Sigil of Earth

Food, Enhancement

  • Food:  Spherified Cilantro Oyster Soup
  • Food:  Dragon's Revelry Starcake (Budget)
  • Food:  Feast of Delectable Birthday Cake (Budget)
  • Enhancement:  Toxic Tuning Crystal
  • Better (prohibitively expensive) food/enhancements are available
    • Snow Diamond Ornament
    •  Writ of Masterful Malice
    •  Writ of Masterful Accuracy
    • Cooking Golem and/or Sharpening Golem (may be freely available on occasion)

Infusions

  •  Malign WvW Infusion can be used for all empty slots (optional)

Build

An alternate set (more Grieving pieces and Krait Runes) is also available and may provide a faster competitive ramp when using Relic of Thorns and other sustain relics. Viper's Variant
Grieving Variant

[&DQIEHyQ+SiWmAAAAswAAAK8AAACpAAAAnAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Rotation

Opener

  1. Signets (Can be cast very early to maintain 5 stacks)
  2. Chant of Action
  3. "To the Limit!"
  • None of these skills will trigger the boss fight, and you can perpetually keep refreshing signet until the fight begins.
  • Signet of Fury and "To the Limit!" provide adrenaline so you may use them in between fight phases or as part of your opener

Skill Priority

Use the following off-cooldown

  1. Flurry (⚔ Burst)
  2. Impale (⚔ 4)
  3. Final Thrust (⚔ 3)
  4. Dolyak Signet
  5. Signet of Might (if required)
  6. Other Signets only if required
  • Try to maintain 4-5 stacks of Signet Mastery.
  • Dolyak Signet has no cast time.
  • Signet of Might has no cast time.

Crowd Control

This build has very poor CC options and you may bring additional CC by sacrificing Dolyak Signet and using other signets instead. Signet Mastery will be harder to maintain.

  1. "On Your Knees!"
  2. Kick
  3. Tremor (swap to mace for CC)

Sustain in Open World

  1. Switch to Healing Signet for baseline healing and to maintain Signet Mastery
  2. Use Relic of the Biomancer for baseline healing
  3. Use Relic of the Mistfire for life siphon and additional damage
  4. Use "We Will Never Yield!" in emergencies
  5. Use Dolyak Signet for stability

entry.title: Signet Sworn Sword [aDPS]
entry.content:
This build relies on the trait Daring Dragon to generate alacrity and stacks of Fierce as Fire to maintain damage. The APM is unfortunately high due to the reduced charge time for Dragon Slash - Force.

Gearing

You may use any combination of Berserker's, Assassin's or Marauder's stats but keep critical chance at 75%

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Assassin's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Peitha

Weapon Options

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Accuracy
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Axe Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Accuracy

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Furious Sharpening Stone (Optimised)
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone (Budget)

Build

[&DQIEHwsVRDunACkP8hoAAKkAAACvAK0A/BqcAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAEWgA2AC8ABQAA]

Notes

This build relies on Dragon Slash - Force (DT1) to deal most of its damage. Because of this, you must not delay Dragon Trigger and you have to use Dragon Slash - Force (DT1) almost immediately after triggering  Relic of Peitha.

Rotation

Precasts

  1. Gunsaber skills
  2. Utilty skills ( Flow Stabilizer x3 [^1]
  3. Mending
  • Use Tactical Reload to get the third Flow Stabilizer

Dragon Slash Combo

You MUST learn this combo:

  1. Dragon Trigger
  2. Flickerstep (triggers  Relic of Peitha)
  3. Dragon Slash - Force (ASAP when charged)
  • Be careful not to cancel your Dragon Trigger by moving!
  • Triggerguard can be added on for free aegis.

Simplified Loop

  1. Dragon Slash Combo
  2. Weapon Skills (5→1→Autos)
  3. Dragon Slash Combo
  4. Gunsaber (4→3→2→1→Autos)
  5. Dragon Slash Combo ↺
  • You may skip Sword 2 or keep the leap as a finisher when you need it.

Crowd Control

This build has weak crowd control. Replace Signet of Might with a physical skills that has CC:

  • Bull's Charge
  • Kick

Physical skills provide a short term buff through Peak Performance, so you can mitigate the damage loss for bringing a CC if you use Dragon Trigger right after using one of the above CC skills.

entry.title: Power Chronomancer [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Power Chronomancer is primarily a combo based rotation, with one longer opener/skill combo and skill pairings.

It is able to do large amounts of CC due to the free elite skill slot, and has multiple utility skills that can be swapped around for a minor DPS loss to provide many utilities, such as reflecting, blinking, portalling, aegis, or stability.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQcBHQoaKDYjDwAAjwEAAM8SAACCAQAAsxIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAvAAA=]

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  4. Signet of the Ether
  5. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  6. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  7. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Signet of the Ether instantly recharges the cooldown of any Phantasmal type skill, and is used to deal more damage.

Use Well of Action off cooldown, as it activates Danger Time, giving you 10% more critical danage. Never delay Well of Action.

Your cooldowns will line up in such a way that you will always be able to pair Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) with either Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) or Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) off cooldown, pairing it with the accompanying skill that is off cooldown.

Every few rotations, your cooldowns will line up such that you will be able to cast the following combo:

Burst Combo:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Signet of the Ether
  4. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  5. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  6. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Which is basically your opener minus the first Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use your burst combo as soon as it is available.

On top of this, you should also use Split Second off cooldown, after the first cast. You should wait until you have 3 clones before casting Split Second for the first time.

Split Second has no cast point, can be casted at the same time as other skills, and will not interrupt any other skill you are currently casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Gravity Well (BIG CC)
  2. Illusionary Leap > Swap (Sword 3)
  3. Time Sink (more clones more CC)

In most situations, just using Gravity Well would be enough contribution to break the CC bar.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can also block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where its cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Video

entry.title: The Bell Ringer [pDPS]
entry.content:
Overview

Classic power reaper spin to win playstyle. Generally speaking, this plays like:

  • Step 1: Prep (with abilities/wells)
  • Step 2: Spin
  • Step 3: Repeat

Gearing

This plays quite tanky and comfy. There are some modifications below to adjust.

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Greatsword Berserker's Force
Hydromancy
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Dragon's
Ring Dragon's
Relic Thief
Relic (alternative) Fireworks

Build

[&DQgTHzIlIj7BEgAAdwEAAPoAAAC9AQAAAxMAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Alternatives and why...

  • Cheaper/non-EoD option: Swap Dragon's for Berserker's
  • Higher risk/higher-rewad: Swap Dragon's for Berserker's
  • Dealing with percentage-based damage? Swap Dragon's for Berserker's
  • Not comfy enough? Swap Scholar runes for Vampirism (only do this for Open World, as it will over-burden your healers in some raid scenarios)

Rotation / Notes

  • Pull enemies to melee range with Spectral Grasp or Grasping Darkness
  • Hit enemies with Death Spiral (Greatsword 3) to inflict vulnerability (and increase your crit chance)
  • Hit enemies with "Chilled to the Bone!" to inflict chill (which grants might, life force and nova explosions every time you hit them)
  • Go into Reaper's Shroud, drop an ice field with Executioner's Scythe
  • Use Soul Spiral in the ice field
  • When out of shroud, focus on life force generation with weapons and "Your Soul Is Mine!"
  • Dual-swords are provided only as a utility option. If you don't own SotO, use axe/warhorn instead.
    • Sword 1/4 will heal
    • Sword 3 provides rapid movement (sacrificing health to move again)
    • Sword 2/5 generate additional life force (note: sacrificing health for second use does not build life force)

Crowd Control

You have several options:

  • Grasping Darkness (Greatsword 5)
  • Executioner's Scythe (Shroud 5)
  • "Chilled to the Bone!"
  • Spectral Grasp

entry.title: Blastmaster [pDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

A Sword priority-based build, with two different priority sets (in/out of forge).

This build is able to deal very high burst damage with no setup required. However, knowing how to save your burst by changing or delaying your rotation requires high levels of familiarity with the build, and if done incorrectly, will lose your more DPS than you would gain.

It also loses out in damage over time, and is weaker on fights with long phases.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Shield Berserker's Impact
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMGNyYfOTcqDwAA5RaGAKMAAABXFgAA8BWJAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgBXAFUAAA==]

Rotation

While playing Power Holosmith, you always want to Engage Photon Forge as soon as it is available, as most of your damage is done during Engage Photon Forge. As Engage Photon Forge replaces your weapon skill bar when it is cast, this rotation will consists of 2 different skill priority orders, during Engage Photon Forge and Holoforge Overheated.

Always cast Rifle Turret before the fight begins, and follow the priority of the mode you are currently in. Auto attack if you have no skills to press.

Engage Photon Forge Priority:

  1. Corona Burst (Forge 3)
  2. Photon Blitz (Forge 4)
  3. Photon Wall --> Launch Wall (above 50% heat)
  4. Laser Disk (above 50% heat)
  5. Prime Light Beam (above 50% heat)
  6. Blade Burst (above 50% heat)

Skip Prime Light Beam if it is on cooldown, and note that Photon Wall has be pressed twice to cast Launch Wall. As there is a delay between Photon Wall flipping over to reveal Launch Wall, I personally like to cast it in the order:

  1. Photon Wall
  2. Laser Disk
  3. Launch Wall

Allowing me to queue skill casts and not lose damage uptime.

Ultimately the order which you press the skills are not important, as long as you are able to squeeze in 2 x Corona Burst (Forge 3), 2 x Photon Blitz (Forge 4), 1 x Launch Wall, 1 x Prime Light Beam, and 1 x Blade Burst for every Engage Photon Forge.

Holoforge Overheated Priority:

  1. Refraction Cutter (Sword 2)
  2. Radiant Arc (Sword 3)

During Holoforge Overheated, you are basically just waiting for Engage Photon Forge to come off cooldown, and pressing any high damage weapon skills to upkeep damage as much as possible.

On top of your rotation, you should also

Cast Off Cooldown:

  1. Particle Accelerator
  2. Surprise Shot

Particle Accelerator and Surprise Shot do not have any cast time, and will not interrupt your current skill.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are:

  1. Static Shock
  2. Magnetic Shield (Shield 4)
  3. Static Shield (Shield 5)

Both Magnetic Shield (Shield 4) and Static Shield (Shield 5) have to be pressed twice for CC, and you can also swap out Rifle Turret for Throw Mine for more CC, and Photon Wall for Personal Battering Ram if you need even more CC.

Magnetic Shield (Shield 4) is also 3s long projectile reflect, while Static Shield (Shield 5) is a 2.5s long block, and can be used as your panic button.

A.E.D. will heal you to 12,280 HP if you take lethal damage, and can also be used as another panic button.

Advanced Tips

Your healing skill, A.E.D., will heal you and remove all damaging conditions when you take lethal damage. This is especially useful in some fights, such as Deimos, where you can skip the mindcrush mechanic, and Sabir, where you can skip both the shockwave and corner mechanic. This will allow you to maintain higher damage uptime on bosses, increasing your DPS.

Video
